title: Complete Sentences
description: Practice building complete sentences with objects. Learn which verbs need objects (transitive) and which don't (intransitive).
learning_objectives: Distinguish transitive verbs (need object) from intransitive (no object)
Build complete SVO sentences with all object gestures
Recognize when a sentence is incomplete

intro_content.title: Complete Your Sentences!
intro_content.explanation: Some verbs NEED an object to make sense (transitive): 'I grab ___'. Others are complete without one (intransitive): 'I stop'. Knowing the difference helps you build correct sentences.

examples.rule: Intransitive: stop is complete without an object

challenges.prompt_spoken: Build the sentence: I drink water.
challenges.context_description: A person drinking a glass of water
challenges.target_concept_explanation: 'Drink' is transitive — it needs an object (what you drink).
hints: Who is drinking? Start with the subject.
What action? Use the DRINK verb gesture.
What are they drinking? Complete with the object.

SUBJECT_I|DRINK.feedback: 'Drink' is transitive — you need to say WHAT you drink. Add an object!
SUBJECT_I|DRINK.rule_reference: Transitive verbs require a direct object.
SUBJECT_I|DRINK.gesture_hint: Add WATER to complete the sentence

challenges.prompt_spoken: Build the sentence: We want book.
challenges.target_concept_explanation: 'We' is 1st person plural. Use the base verb form 'want' (not 'wants').
hints: 'We' means more than one person. Use the WE gesture.
What do we want? Finish with the object.

SUBJECT_WE|WANTS|BOOK.feedback: 'We' is plural! Use the base form 'want', not 'wants'. The S-form is only for He/She/It.
SUBJECT_WE|WANTS|BOOK.rule_reference: Plural subjects (We, They) use base form verbs.

key_takeaways: Transitive verbs (grab, eat, drink, want) need an object
Intransitive verbs (stop, go) are complete without an object
Plural subjects (We, They) use base verb forms
Always check: is my sentence complete?
common_mistakes: Leaving out the object with transitive verbs
Adding unnecessary objects to intransitive verbs
